child.name: resourceType
child.description: This is a Bundle resource

child.name: id
child.description: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

child.name: meta
child.description: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not alwa...
child.longDesc: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: versionId
child.description: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is create...
child.longDesc: The version specific identifier, as it appears in the version portion of the URL. This value changes when the resource is created, updated, or deleted.

child.name: lastUpdated
child.description: When the resource last changed - e.g. when the version changed.

child.name: source
child.description: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can...
child.longDesc: A uri that identifies the source system of the resource. This provides a minimal amount of [[[Provenance]]] information that can be used to track or differentiate the source of information in the resource. The source may identify another FHIR server, document, message, database, etc.

child.name: profile
child.description: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a ref...
child.longDesc: A list of profiles (references to [[[StructureDefinition]]] resources) that this resource claims to conform to. The URL is a reference to [[[StructureDefinition.url]]].

child.name: security
child.description: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructur...
child.longDesc: Security labels applied to this resource. These tags connect specific resources to the overall security policy and infrastructure.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: tag
child.description: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applic...
child.longDesc: Tags applied to this resource. Tags are intended to be used to identify and relate resources to process and workflow, and applications are not required to consider the tags when interpreting the meaning of a resource.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: implicitRules
child.description: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing...
child.longDesc: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

child.name: language
child.description: The base language in which the resource is written.

child.name: identifier
child.description: A persistent identifier for the bundle that won't change as a bundle is copied from server to server.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: type
child.description: Indicates the purpose of this bundle - how it is intended to be used.

child.name: timestamp
child.description: The date/time that the bundle was assembled - i.e. when the resources were placed in the bundle.

child.name: total
child.description: If a set of search matches, this is the (potentially estimated) total number of entries of type 'match' across all pages in the ...
child.longDesc: If a set of search matches, this is the (potentially estimated) total number of entries of type 'match' across all pages in the search. It does not include search.mode = 'include' or 'outcome' entries and it does not provide a count of the number of entries in the Bundle.

child.name: link
child.description: A series of links that provide context to this bundle.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: relation
child.description: A name which details the functional use for this link - see [http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml...
child.longDesc: A name which details the functional use for this link - see [http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml#link-relations-1](http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml#link-relations-1).

child.name: url
child.description: The reference details for the link.

child.name: entry
child.description: An entry in a bundle resource - will either contain a resource or information about a resource (transactions and history only).

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: link
child.description: A series of links that provide context to this entry.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: relation
child.description: A name which details the functional use for this link - see [http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml...
child.longDesc: A name which details the functional use for this link - see [http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml#link-relations-1](http://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml#link-relations-1).

child.name: url
child.description: The reference details for the link.

child.name: fullUrl
child.description: The Absolute URL for the resource. Except for transactions and batches, each entry in a Bundle must have a fullUrl. The fullUrl ...
child.longDesc: The Absolute URL for the resource. Except for transactions and batches, each entry in a Bundle must have a fullUrl. The fullUrl SHALL NOT disagree with the id in the resource - i.e. if the fullUrl is not a urn:uuid, the URL shall be version-independent URL consistent with the Resource.id. The fullUrl is a version independent reference to the resource. Even when not required, fullUrl MAY be set to a urn:uuid to allow referencing entries in a transaction. The fullUrl can be an arbitrary URI and is not limited to urn:uuid, urn:oid, http, and https. The fullUrl element SHALL have a value except when: * invoking a create * invoking or responding to an operation where the body is not a single identified resource * invoking or returning the results of a search or history operation.

child.name: search
child.description: Information about the search process that lead to the creation of this entry.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: mode
child.description: Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey infor...
child.longDesc: Why this entry is in the result set - whether it's included as a match or because of an _include requirement, or to convey information or warning information about the search process.

child.name: score
child.description: When searching, the server's search ranking score for the entry.

child.name: request
child.description: Additional information about how this entry should be processed as part of a transaction or batch. For history, it shows how th...
child.longDesc: Additional information about how this entry should be processed as part of a transaction or batch. For history, it shows how the entry was processed to create the version contained in the entry.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: method
child.description: In a transaction or batch, this is the HTTP action to be executed for this entry. In a history bundle, this indicates the HTTP a...
child.longDesc: In a transaction or batch, this is the HTTP action to be executed for this entry. In a history bundle, this indicates the HTTP action that occurred.

child.name: url
child.description: The URL for this entry, relative to the root (the address to which the request is posted).

child.name: ifNoneMatch
child.description: If the ETag values match, return a 304 Not Modified status. See the API documentation for ["Conditional Read"](http.html#cread).

child.name: ifModifiedSince
child.description: Only perform the operation if the last updated date matches. See the API documentation for ["Conditional Read"](http.html#cread)...
child.longDesc: Only perform the operation if the last updated date matches. See the API documentation for ["Conditional Read"](http.html#cread).

child.name: ifMatch
child.description: Only perform the operation if the Etag value matches. For more information, see the API section ["Managing Resource Contention"]...
child.longDesc: Only perform the operation if the Etag value matches. For more information, see the API section ["Managing Resource Contention"](http.html#concurrency).

child.name: ifNoneExist
child.description: Instruct the server not to perform the create if a specified resource already exists. For further information, see the API docum...
child.longDesc: Instruct the server not to perform the create if a specified resource already exists. For further information, see the API documentation for ["Conditional Create"](http.html#ccreate). This is just the query portion of the URL - what follows the "?" (not including the "?").

child.name: response
child.description: Indicates the results of processing the corresponding 'request' entry in the batch or transaction being responded to or what the...
child.longDesc: Indicates the results of processing the corresponding 'request' entry in the batch or transaction being responded to or what the results of an operation where when returning history.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: status
child.description: The status code returned by processing this entry. The status SHALL start with a 3 digit HTTP code (e.g. 404) and may contain th...
child.longDesc: The status code returned by processing this entry. The status SHALL start with a 3 digit HTTP code (e.g. 404) and may contain the standard HTTP description associated with the status code.

child.name: location
child.description: The location header created by processing this operation, populated if the operation returns a location.

child.name: etag
child.description: The Etag for the resource, if the operation for the entry produced a versioned resource (see [Resource Metadata and Versioning](...
child.longDesc: The Etag for the resource, if the operation for the entry produced a versioned resource (see [Resource Metadata and Versioning](http.html#versioning) and [Managing Resource Contention](http.html#concurrency)).

child.name: lastModified
child.description: The date/time that the resource was modified on the server.

child.name: signature
child.description: Digital Signature - base64 encoded. XML-DSig or a JWS.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: type
child.description: An indication of the reason that the entity signed this document. This may be explicitly included as part of the signature infor...
child.longDesc: An indication of the reason that the entity signed this document. This may be explicitly included as part of the signature information and can be used when determining accountability for various actions concerning the document.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: when
child.description: When the digital signature was signed.

child.name: who
child.description: A reference to an application-usable description of the identity that signed (e.g. the signature used their private key).

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: onBehalfOf
child.description: A reference to an application-usable description of the identity that is represented by the signature.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: reference
child.description: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is r...
child.longDesc: A reference to a location at which the other resource is found. The reference may be a relative reference, in which case it is relative to the service base URL, or an absolute URL that resolves to the location where the resource is found. The reference may be version specific or not. If the reference is not to a FHIR RESTful server, then it should be assumed to be version specific. Internal fragment references (start with '#') refer to contained resources.

child.name: type
child.description: The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.ref...
child.longDesc:
The expected type of the target of the reference. If both Reference.type and Reference.reference are populated and Reference.reference is a FHIR URL, both SHALL be consistent.
The type is the Canonical URL of Resource Definition that is the type this reference refers to. References are URLs that are relative to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/ e.g. "Patient" is a reference to http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Patient. Absolute URLs are only allowed for logical models (and can only be used in references in logical models, not resources).

child.name: identifier
child.description: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either becaus...
child.longDesc: An identifier for the target resource. This is used when there is no way to reference the other resource directly, either because the entity it represents is not available through a FHIR server, or because there is no way for the author of the resource to convert a known identifier to an actual location. There is no requirement that a Reference.identifier point to something that is actually exposed as a FHIR instance, but it SHALL point to a business concept that would be expected to be exposed as a FHIR instance, and that instance would need to be of a FHIR resource type allowed by the reference.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: use
child.description: The purpose of this identifier.

child.name: type
child.description: A coded type for the identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: coding
child.description: A reference to a code defined by a terminology system.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: system
child.description: The identification of the code system that defines the meaning of the symbol in the code.

child.name: version
child.description: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the...
child.longDesc: The version of the code system which was used when choosing this code. Note that a well-maintained code system does not need the version reported, because the meaning of codes is consistent across versions. However this cannot consistently be assured, and when the meaning is not guaranteed to be consistent, the version SHOULD be exchanged.

child.name: code
child.description: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding...
child.longDesc: A symbol in syntax defined by the system. The symbol may be a predefined code or an expression in a syntax defined by the coding system (e.g. post-coordination).

child.name: display
child.description: A representation of the meaning of the code in the system, following the rules of the system.

child.name: userSelected
child.description: Indicates that this coding was chosen by a user directly - e.g. off a pick list of available items (codes or displays).

child.name: text
child.description: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents...
child.longDesc: A human language representation of the concept as seen/selected/uttered by the user who entered the data and/or which represents the intended meaning of the user.

child.name: system
child.description: Establishes the namespace for the value - that is, an absolute URL that describes a set values that are unique.

child.name: value
child.description: The portion of the identifier typically relevant to the user and which is unique within the context of the system.

child.name: period
child.description: Time period during which identifier is/was valid for use.

child.name: id
child.description: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces...
child.longDesc: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

child.name: start
child.description: The start of the period. The boundary is inclusive.

child.name: end
child.description: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was cr...
child.longDesc: The end of the period. If the end of the period is missing, it means no end was known or planned at the time the instance was created. The start may be in the past, and the end date in the future, which means that period is expected/planned to end at that time.

child.name: assigner
child.description: Organization that issued/manages the identifier.

child.name: display
child.description: Plain text narrative that identifies the resource in addition to the resource reference.

child.name: targetFormat
child.description: A mime type that indicates the technical format of the target resources signed by the signature.

child.name: sigFormat
child.description: A mime type that indicates the technical format of the signature. Important mime types are application/signature+xml for X ML Di...
child.longDesc: A mime type that indicates the technical format of the signature. Important mime types are application/signature+xml for X ML DigSig, application/jose for JWS, and image/* for a graphical image of a signature, etc.

child.name: data
child.description: The base64 encoding of the Signature content. When signature is not recorded electronically this element would be empty.
